findings.description: Instructional infographic is reformatted from a compact graphic layout into a stacked text-heavy layout.
findings.evidence: In the PDF, page 1 is a centered infographic with three visually grouped vertical panels connected to a central circle. In the HTML, the same content is shown as the image followed by separate text sections beneath it, changing the original whitespace grouping and page composition.

findings.description: Some visual emphasis and graphic-based formatting from the infographic is not preserved in the text rendition.
findings.evidence: The PDF uses large circular headings, icons, and integrated callouts for 'Guidelines', 'Printing', and 'Before Sending'. In the HTML, these are converted into plain headings and bullet lists below the image, reducing the original visual hierarchy though the text remains present.

findings.description: Heading hierarchy does not accurately reflect the document structure and uses multiple h1 elements.
findings.evidence:
HTML uses

Do Not Include This Page

on page 1 and

Motor Fuel Backup Tax Return

on page 2, resulting in more than one h1. It also promotes form labels such as "Fill in applicable circles:", "Account Number", "Return for Month of", "MAIL TO:", and "QUESTIONS:" to h3 headings, though in the PDF these are form labels/side boxes rather than section headings.

findings.description: One image alt text is inaccurate/generic relative to the actual image content.
findings.evidence: HTML includes an image on page 2 with alt="NCDOR Logo", but the PDF image area appears to contain the NCDOR wordmark with adjacent form branding/header content rather than a standalone logo. Another page-2 image has empty alt text despite containing visible header graphics.
